title: CTM Refabricated
body:
<center>

## CTM Refabricated

[![sodium incompat](https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1073717602880327761/1091419443487322193/sodium_vector.svg)](https://modrinth.com/mod/indium)
<hr>

### 📖 About 📖
<hr>
</center>

Welcome to CTM Refabricated, a port of ConnectedTexturesMod for Fabric which is a port of the ConnectedTexturesMod (a mouthful, I know)

CTM Refabricated allows resource packs to add connected textures, emissive rendering, and much more. A resource pack which adds these effects to vanilla, and some select mods, can be found [here](https://minecraft.curseforge.com/projects/ctp).

Note: CTM Refabricated does not support the Optifine CTM format, for that, use Continuity.

In compliance with the GPL license, I state the following:

The original mod can be found [here](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/ctm-fabric).

This mod was forked from the source code found [here](https://github.com/PepperCode1/ConnectedTexturesMod-Fabric).

My changes consist of porting the mod to 1.18.2 and 1.19 Fabric and editing code where necessary to use the updated methods in those versions

<center>
<hr>

### ⚠️ WARNING ⚠️
<hr>
</center>

## BEYOND 1.19.2, THIS PROJECT IS ABANDONED

This mod is not very efficient. This is meant as a port of CTM, so resourcepacks built on the CTM mod by tterag can run on Fabric.
However, if you are a mod developer looking to make a cross-platform solution to your connected textures needs, this is not the best
way to go about it. 

If you're a fabric/quilt exclusive developer who doesn't necessarily need access to all the formats CTM has to offer (like columns
or random) I recommend checking out [Continuity](https://modrinth.com/mod/continuity) 

If you are in need of the formats CTM offers or if you're developing a cross-platform mod on 1.19.4 and beyond, I recommend checking
out our other project, [Athena](https://modrinth.com/mod/athena-ctm)! It offers alot of the same features that CTM provided, while
being much more performant on both Fabric and Forge than either CTM or CTM Refabricated.

<hr>
